narrative_text: THOUGH THE DEVICE MALFUNCTIONED, THE CLINICIAN WAS ABLE TO PERFORM BIOPSIES WITH NO INJURY TO THE PATIENT. HOWEVER, BECAUSE OF THE HARDWARE ERROR, THE PATIENT PROBABLY EXPERIENCED A DELAY IN THE PROCEDURE WHICH CAUSED THEM TO HAVE ADDITIONAL EXPOSURE TIME TO RADIATION AND ADDITIONAL TIME UNDER ANESTHESIA. THUS, IN CONCLUSION, THOUGH THE HARDWARE MALFUNCTIONED AND CAUSED ADDITIONAL TIME UNDER ANESTHESIA AND ADDITIONAL EXPOSURE TO RADIATION BODY VISION MEDICAL LTD. IS FILING THIS ADVERSE EVENT REPORT IN AN ABUNDANCE OF CAUTION. DURING THE PROCEDURE THE COMPUTER POWER WAS REPEATEDLY LOST. THE USER LOST LESION IMAGE AND LOCATION AND COULD NOT CONTINUE SAMPLING FROM THE ACCURATE LOCATION. BIOPSIES WERE TAKEN BUT THE CASE WAS EVENTUALLY ABORTED AND NOT FINISHED AS PLANNED.
human_notes: Device-specific link appears valid, but the narrative mainly describes hardware/device malfunction rather than AI/software-output behavior.

narrative_text: AFTER SUCCESSFULLY COMPLETING THE FIRST PROCEDURE OF THE DAY, DR. HOGARTH WAS WAITING FOR THE PROCEDURE APPLICATION TO FINISH PROCESSING THE CASE DATA AND CLOSE. WHILE WAITING FOR THIS PROCESS TO COMPLETE THE CLINICIAN PERFORMED THE HARD SHUTDOWN OF THE TABLET AND THEN RESTARTED IT WITHOUT RESTARTING THE WHOLE SYSTEM, INCLUDING TABLET AND MAIN UNIT, AS RECOMMENDED BY VENDOR. THIS CAUSED THE TABLET AND MAIN UNIT TO BECOME OUT OF SYNC IN THE SUBSEQUENT TWO CASES THAT DAY. DURING CASES 2 AND 3, FALSE CABT ERROR MESSAGES WERE DISPLAYED DURING MAIN CARINA REGISTRATION. THE CLINICIAN WAS ABLE TO CONTINUE THROUGH THE ERROR MESSAGES AND PROCEEDED TO MARK THE MAIN CARINA. AFTER THIS, THE LESION OVERLAY WAS INITIALLY DISPLAYED ON THE FLUORO VIDEO DURING PROCEDURE; HOWEVER, AS SOON AS THE CLINICIAN MADE HIS FIRST ISOCENTER ADJUSTMENT, THE OVERLAY DISAPPEARED AND NEVER CAME BACK. HE TRIED MULTIPLE VIEWING ANGLES IN AN ATTEMPT TO GET THE OVERLAY TO REAPPEAR BUT IT DID NOT.
